GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "The 5 Australian labels you need in your wardrobe for the season ahead", "Embrace a resort mentality in easy, on-trend pieces from these local designers", "For all the praise that a European summer holiday gets, no one quite does warm weather like we do right here in Australia. It's something we've always known, but has become even more pertinent this year as we revel on home soil for a while longer yet (lucky us). It's fitting that our born-and-bred local designers are adept at creating pieces we want to live in all summer-long: during daylight-savings-drenched afternoons, backyard BBQs, and swims that somehow end up stretching over the course of the day. As the weather warms up and the idea of switching on an out-of-office gets closer to being a reality, here are five Australian designers whose SS20 collections are worthy of your wardrobe — a curation all available at David Jones. 1. Peony Swimwear For most Australians, swimwear isn't simply relegated to being by a body of water, and Burleigh-based designer Peony has perfectly tapped into the mentality that pervades our way of living and dressing in summer. Their range of bikinis and one-pieces are beloved for their dainty, feminine prints (think florals, spots, and stripes) and flattering cuts. 2. Aje Aje's characteristically playful Resort 21 collection is full of all the whimsy you've come to expect from the brand, in fresh botanical hues such as soft pinks, elegant bottle greens, plus touches of peach and terracotta. Consider it your go-to for a party look worthy of your next special occasion. 3. St Agni St Agni's speciality lies in the pieces you go back to time and time again during the summer months: versatile shirting, relaxed trousers and easy wrap styles in breathable fabrics such as linen, plus quality handmade leather accessories. And in a classic palette of creams, natural browns and black, their pieces can easily be worn back with the rest of your wardrobe. 4. Bec and Bridge Whether its pool-ready swimwear, a party-apropos mini, or a wear-anywhere peasant blouse you're after, beloved Aussie brand Bec and Bridge has you covered. As always, their pieces will have you feeling on trend and in the mood for warmer days ahead. 5. Bassike Bassike's elevated take on everyday wear is a staple in most Australians' wardrobes, and the brand's Spring '20 range has updates that feel fresh for the season ahead. Shop citrusy yellows and lime greens, or double down on the tie-dye print in pieces that effortlessly tap into one of the moment's biggest trends. If all this talk of summer days ahead has you feeling inspired to hone your own resort wardrobe, tune into the David Jones Virtual Runway presented by American Express this Friday night, as part of the 2020 Vogue American Express Fashion's Night In (our huge 11-day virtual celebration of fashion, beauty and shopping). The virtual fashion show will be a first for the iconic Australian department store, and all the faces you know and love will be there — including leading Australian models Georgia Fowler, Akiima, Cameron Stephens and Zoe Banard — in new season SS20 pieces from the likes of Aje, St Agni, Peony, Bassike, Bondi Born, Maticevski, Isabel Marant, Marni and Balenciaga. Set at the iconic Seidler House in the stunning NSW Southern Highlands, and fittingly themed "A place in the sun," it's the perfect way to kick off an exciting season ahead with resort-ready looks for all occasions. The best bit? All the pieces you love — from David Jones' exclusive range of Australian and international designers — will be instantly shoppable."
